summary_one_line: An xmlns namespace declaration whose name has no matching DTD attribute declaration, reached through the multi-byte 'xmlns' prefix-match cascade in SAX2.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML document with a DTD (DOCTYPE/SYSTEM) and a namespace declaration (xmlns:prefix) whose attribute name is NOT declared in the DTD's attribute table, so the xmlGetDtdQAttrDesc/xmlGetDtdAttrDesc lookups all return NULL and xmlValidateOneNamespace sees attrDecl == NULL. Reaching the validation call first requires passing the byte-exact 'xmlns' prefix comparison cascade (att[0..4] == 'x','m','l','n','s') in the SAX2 attribute path.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch in 8/10 trials. Its winning seeds carry a DOCTYPE/SYSTEM DTD and reach xmlValidateOneNamespace with attrDecl NULL: on the d=1 chain the blocker line L4660 shows W taking both sides (T=9 F=9) while the loser only ever takes the false side (T=0 F=27). The CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards partial matches on the multi-byte 'xmlns' equality cascades, letting the winner climb att[0]=='x'..att[4]=='s' (L1235/L1236, L1724/L1725) which W hits (T=18 F=0 / T=9 F=0) but the loser stalls on (T=6 F=6, T=0 F=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves the branch in 0/10 trials, blocked in 8. cmplog has the same I2S mutator but lacks value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient, so it cannot accumulate partial-credit feedback on the conjunctive 'xmlns' byte comparisons: at L1724 the loser is T=0 F=0 (never reaches the test true) and at L1235 it is split T=6 F=6, never driving the namespace-validation call where attrDecl == NULL can be observed taking its true side (L4660 loser T=0 F=27).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets on intercepted CMPs) is the single technique delta between value_profile_cmplog (winner) and cmplog (loser); both already have the same input-to-state substitution mutator, so that comparison-solving capability is held constant. The gradient gives partial-match feedback on the multi-byte 'xmlns' equality cascade, letting the winner assemble the namespace-decl path that the loser cannot.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog > cmplog with the technique delta being value_profile, holding I2S constant.
The winner resolves the branch in 8/10 trials while the loser resolves it in 0/10.
The loser never resolves the branch.
The blocker is the attrDecl == NULL true branch in xmlValidateOneNamespace.
Reaching the validation call requires DTD attribute lookups (xmlGetDtdQAttrDesc / xmlGetDtdAttrDesc) all returning NULL so attrDecl stays NULL.
On the blocker line the winner takes both sides while the loser only takes the false side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L4660  T=9 F=9  T=0 F=27  if (attrDecl == NULL) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winner clears the multi-byte 'xmlns' prefix comparison cascade in xmlSAX2StartElement that the loser never reaches true.
The winner passes the conjunctive ns[0..5] == 'xmlns'\0 test in xmlSAX2AttributeInternal while the loser stalls split.
The winner inputs contain a DOCTYPE SYSTEM DTD declaration in their bytes.
value_profile_cmplog's instrumentation adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of the CMP buffer.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's feedback is the same edge-bucket signal as naive, with no CMP_MAP gradient.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Feedback**: same edge-bucket signal as naive. The CMP buffer is
consumed by the mutator, not by feedback.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile delta), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory I2S lineage check does not apply. Both fuzzers in the decisive pair (value_profile_cmplog and cmplog) already carry I2SRandReplace per MECHANISM CONTEXT, so I2S is held constant and the divergence is attributed to value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback; no dash-row lineage claim is made.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same xmlns-prefix equality cascade gating an undeclared-attribute path showing cmplog (no value profile) resolving it as often as value_profile_cmplog, i.e. no dose-response separation when the CMP_MAP gradient is the only varied factor.

weakest_evidence_point: No seed lineage was inspected to confirm that the winning seeds' resolving mutations were driven by gradient-guided byte assembly rather than lucky havoc, so the gradient attribution rests on the structural CMP cascade plus the cmplog-vs-vpc feedback delta alone.
